summary: Session 6 (final session of Set 077) closes with the coordinated release published. UAT: the checklist was rewritten wholesale to the Set 078 authoring bar (literal HumanAction/Expectation, live-dogfooded CLI recipes for the pending-verification banner and Mode-B start-time gate, source-re-grounded strings elsewhere) after the operator suspended the original walk mid-session over instruction quality; two real drifts from source were caught and fixed in the rewrite (a stale button label; a false claim that the Getting Started doc's tier callout differs by verification mode -- it is tier-only). The operator re-walked the rewritten checklist for real: 14/14 items Passes=true, zero feedback on any Other row. Both README.md files (root and the extension's Marketplace listing) were reviewed against the actually-shipped 0.27.0/0.34.0 feature set and updated for concrete gaps (three-way setup choice, Python prerequisite check, canonical cross-provider-verification.md + self-completing Evaluate prompts, pending-verification banner, Start Next Session auto-routing). A pre-existing CI failure left by Set 078's last two commits (cold-start golden-fixture drift after a template addition) was found and fixed (UPDATE_GOLDEN=1; diff confirmed as exactly the expected addition, nothing else) before this session's own commit could go green. With Test green, the operator authorized and the release executed: v0.27.0 tagged and pushed (first attempt mis-tagged the wrong commit -- caught by the release workflow's own tag/pyproject.toml version-match gate -- corrected by re-tagging commit 51fc437, the commit where the 0.27.0 bump actually landed and which already carried a recorded green Test run), then vsix-v0.34.0 (existing VSCE_PAT still valid, no renewal needed), then v0.28.0 for Set 078 per the release-ordering invariant. All three GitHub Actions workflows completed successfully; independently confirmed via the live PyPI JSON API that both 0.27.0 and 0.28.0 are present. docs/repository-reference.md's release-status table and version-walk entries were updated from 'publish pending' language to actual run ids/tagged commits/dates; Set 078's own change-log.md was updated the same way; Set 077's change-log.md was authored from scratch (did not exist before this session's close). Required end-of-set path-aware critique (2+ providers, clean) and version bumps/CHANGELOGs were already completed in an earlier pass of this same session (commit 51fc437) and are unchanged. Cross-provider session-verification of this session's own continuation work (the UAT rewrite, README updates, CI fix, and release/documentation) returned VERIFIED with only non-blocking nits after two retries that correctly caught process gaps in the verification itself (an initial pass omitted uncommitted working-tree files from the diff and asserted release claims without attaching the raw evidence backing them; both were fixed by re-running with the corrected diff and the actual pasted gh/git/PyPI command output) -- see s6-verification-round-5.md for the clean verdict and s6-verification-round-2/3/4.md for the superseded attempts, kept for the audit trail per this repo's own 'never edit the saved review file' convention. A real follow-on scope item surfaced during this close: the Copilot CLI hybrid tier (Set 078) has no representation anywhere in the Getting Started onboarding form, so a Copilot-locked operator has no way to discover it exists; combined with a request to simplify the Lightweight verification-mode radio copy, this is planned as a new session set rather than folded into this close.
